summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a single orphan WebSocket continuation frame (opcode 0) sent immediately after the HTTP upgrade handshake crashes the facil.io server with a NULL pointer dereference in fiobj_str_write, triggered via websocket_on_unwrapped.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the facil.io server process to crash with SIGSEGV when the crafted continuation frame reaches fiobj_str_write with FIOBJ_INVALID (NULL), producing a denial-of-service condition.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the facil.io WebSocket server, performed the HTTP upgrade handshake, sent a single masked continuation frame (opcode 0x00), and confirmed the server process crashed. The exploit client observed a connection reset (BrokenPipeError/ConnectionResetError), confirming the SIGSEGV in the server.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts the server in the background, waits for the readiness marker, runs the exploit client, and checks that the server process is no longer alive after the exploit. The exploit client confirms the crash by detecting that the TCP connection was reset (server-side SIGSEGV).
evidence_produced_by_run: The server process exited abnormally (killed by signal) after receiving the orphan continuation frame.
The exploit client confirmed connection loss (ConnectionResetError) indicating the server crashed.
The server log shows the WebSocket upgrade was completed before the crash, confirming the vulnerable code path was reached.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag v0.7.4, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
